caption: Europe has “maybe six weeks or so (of) jet fuel left,” the head of the International Energy Agency said Thursday in a wide-ranging Associated Press interview, warning of possible flight cancellations “soon” if oil supplies remain blocked by the Iran war.   #europe #energy #AP #interview
transcript: In Europe, we have maybe six weeks or so of jet fuel left. If we are not able to open the Strait of Hormuz, if the refineries as a result of start to work, I can tell you soon we will hear the news that some of the flights from city A to city B might be canceled as a result of lack of jet fuel. So two big problems, first the high prices and second, availability of the gas, availability of the jet fuel, diesel and others. If the Hormuz is not open until end of May, many countries, starting from the weaker economies, are going to face huge challenges and this will go from the high inflation numbers to coming close to slow growth or even to recession in some cases. So this is the reason I very much hope that before the end of May, we see the Hormuz Strait is opened.

rewrite: The head of the International Energy Agency said Europe has approximately six weeks of jet fuel remaining. He stated that if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed and refineries cannot operate, some flights may be canceled due to jet fuel shortages. He identified two main concerns: high prices and availability of fuel products including gas, jet fuel, and diesel. He said that if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by the end of May, countries with weaker economies will face challenges including high inflation, slow growth, or recession.
